identity.name: Cloud App — Microsoft Admin-Consent-Required Scope Request (T1078.004)
identity.summary: An OAuth authorize request to the Microsoft Identity Platform (login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize) requests Directory.ReadWrite.All and Application.ReadWrite.All — scopes that require tenant admin consent. Cortex Cloud App Security flags any non-admin identity soliciting an admin-consent-required scope, treats Application.ReadWrite.All in the same grant body as a tenant-take…
identity.description: Admin-consent-required Microsoft Graph scopes such as Directory.ReadWrite.All and Application.ReadWrite.All cannot be self-granted by an end user — they hand an app tenant-wide control over directory objects and app registrations, the exact primitive used to plant a backdoor service principal for durable cloud persistence. This scenario drives oauth_grant_emulator against the Microsoft v2.0 authorize endpoint with the admin_consent scope preset, requesting Directory.ReadWrite.All and Application.ReadWrite.All. Detection is on the audit-event shape: Cortex Cloud App Security flags any grant where consent_type is user (or unconsented) yet the requested oauth_scopes include an admin-consent-required scope — an impossible self-grant that signals consent-phishing or a rogue app registration. A second BIOC fires when Application.ReadWrite.All appears in the same grant body (the highest-risk app-management scope). A repeat attempt is treated as persistence intent rather than a one-off mistake, and the NGFW EAL plane corroborates the outbound /oauth2/v2.0/authorize request to login.microsoftonline.com. A correlation rule fuses the scope-request, app-management, and repeat signals into one cloud-persistence incident. The request uses a bogus client_id against the common tenant, so the authorize endpoint returns a 4xx and no consent is granted — detection happens on the request, not a successful grant.

prerequisites.description: oauth_grant_emulator supports the microsoft provider and Cortex Cloud App Security ingests Microsoft 365 unified audit / Entra sign-in logs.
Outbound HTTPS egress to login.microsoftonline.com passes through the in-path NGFW EAL stack.

payload.code:
# Admin-consent-required scope request against the Microsoft v2.0 authorize endpoint — bogus client_id, no consent granted.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/cloud-002.yml --live
# Repeat to demonstrate deterministic persistence-intent signalling.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/cloud-002-rep.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Microsoft audit event requesting an admin-consent-required scope from a non-admin identity.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: dataset = msft_o365_audit and consent_type != "admin" and oauth_scopes contains "Directory.ReadWrite.All"

expected_artifacts.description: Application.ReadWrite.All present in the same grant body.

biocs.name: CLOUD-002 Pre-Flight Microsoft Provider Supported
biocs.description: Pre-flight: provider list includes microsoft. Confirms the oauth_grant_emulator params schema advertises the microsoft provider before any grant is emitted.
biocs.logic:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter event_type = "eal_plugin_describe" or action contains "oauth_grant_emulator"
| filter app_name = "oauth_grant_emulator"
| fields _time, user, app_name, action

biocs.name: CLOUD-002 Microsoft Application ReadWrite All In Same Grant Body
biocs.description: Cloud App — Application.ReadWrite.All in same grant body. The highest-risk app-management Graph scope appears in the authorize request, enabling rogue service-principal creation for tenant persistence.
biocs.logic:
dataset = msft_o365_audit
| filter oauth_scopes contains "Application.ReadWrite.All"
| filter consent_type != "admin"
| filter client_id != null
| fields _time, user, app_name, client_id, oauth_scopes, consent_type, source_ip

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-002 Microsoft Directory ReadWrite All Admin-Consent Scope Request
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_o365_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter consent_type != "admin"
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("Directory.ReadWrite.All", ".default")
| filter client_id != null
| fields _time, user, app_name, client_id, oauth_scopes, consent_type, source_ip, geo

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-002 NGFW Microsoft v2 Authorize Admin-Consent Egress
xql_queries.query:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "login.microsoftonline.com"
| filter uri_path contains "/oauth2/v2.0/authorize"
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("Directory.ReadWrite.All", "Application.ReadWrite.All")
| fields _time, user, dst_host, uri_path, oauth_scopes, source_ip

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-002 Microsoft Repeat Admin-Consent Persistence Attempt
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_o365_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter consent_type != "admin"
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("Directory.ReadWrite.All", "Application.ReadWrite.All")
| comp count() as attempts by user, client_id, app_name
| filter attempts >= 2
| fields user, client_id, app_name, attempts

correlation_rules.name: Microsoft Admin-Consent OAuth Abuse Cloud Persistence — Same Identity
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(CLOUD-002 Microsoft Directory ReadWrite All Admin-Consent Scope Request) OR BIOC(CLOUD-002 Microsoft Application ReadWrite All In Same Grant Body) OR XQL(CLOUD-002 Microsoft Repeat Admin-Consent Persistence Attempt)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud App Security — Non-admin identity requests admin-consent-required Graph scope
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Repeated admin-consent grant attempts indicate persistence intent

products.evidence_query: dataset = msft_o365_audit | filter oauth_scopes contains "Directory.ReadWrite.All" and consent_type != "admin" | fields _time, user, app_name, oauth_scopes
Correlation CR-CLOUDAPP-0002 fuses the admin-consent scope request, Application.ReadWrite.All app-management scope, and repeat attempts into one cloud-persistence incident.

use_cases.name: Admin-Consent OAuth Abuse Detection
use_cases.description: Detect a non-admin identity requesting admin-consent-required Microsoft Graph scopes and correlate repeat attempts into a cloud-persistence incident.

test_cases.objective: An admin-consent scope request from a non-admin identity fires the Cloud App detection.
preconditions: oauth_grant_emulator microsoft provider available and M365 audit logs ingested.
Detections from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe the admin-consent and Application.ReadWrite.All alerts.
success_criteria: XQL `CLOUD-002 Microsoft Directory ReadWrite All Admin-Consent Scope Request` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `CLOUD-002 Microsoft Application ReadWrite All In Same Grant Body` fires.

test_cases.objective: A repeat attempt confirms persistence intent and stitches into one incident.
success_criteria: XQL `CLOUD-002 Microsoft Repeat Admin-Consent Persistence Attempt` returns >= 1 row.

preventive_controls: Disable user consent for apps; require admin consent workflow for all third-party apps.
Restrict who can register applications and consent on behalf of the tenant in Entra ID.
Alert on and review any app granted Directory.ReadWrite.All or Application.ReadWrite.All.
detection_engineering: Flag any grant where the requested scope requires admin consent but the consenting identity is not an admin.
Treat Application.ReadWrite.All as a tenant-takeover-risk scope and alert at critical severity.
Correlate repeat admin-consent attempts from the same identity/app as persistence intent.

references.title: Microsoft - Volt Typhoon OAuth Persistence Patterns
